openingQuery: Can you connect me with a real stylist? My friend just lost her mother and I want a person to help me pick a sympathy gift, not just see product cards.

chat.content: Can you connect me with a real stylist? My friend just lost her mother and I want a person to help me pick a sympathy gift, not just see product cards.

I'm so sorry for your friend's loss. I've passed your note to a Pellier stylist who'll reach out within one business day to help you choose something thoughtful — they'll consider your friend's relationship, the moment, and what arrives well. The catalog couldn't read this room; a stylist can.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → Curator → escalate_to_stylist (sympathy framing, honest fallback)

toolCalls.description: Hand off to a human stylist for sympathy/sentimental gifting beyond catalog scope
toolCalls.resultSummary: Stylist handoff queued · response window 1 business day

meta.note: Curator's hybrid retrieval can find candles all day — but the right sympathy gift depends on the friendship, not the catalog. Escalation is the honest answer.

telemetry.title: Intent → Curator
telemetry.description: 'real stylist' + 'sympathy gift' → orchestrator stylist-handoff branch routes to recommendation

telemetry.title: Skill loaded

telemetry.description: Claude Opus 4.6 · 0.4 — empathy first, concrete next step second

brief.headline: Sympathy is a job for a person

sections.title: Why hybrid + rerank is the wrong tool here
paragraphs: Anna's other four turns lean on hybrid + Cohere Rerank because gifting queries blend vibe with literal constraints, and rerank scores both. But sympathy gifting isn't a vibe — it's a relationship. The right object depends on whether her friend is a colleague or a sister, on whether the loss was sudden or expected, on what the friend has loved before.
No retrieval pipeline holds that context. The catalog can return tasteful candles; only a person can read the room.

sections.title: Escalation as architecture, not failure
paragraphs: Every specialist ships with escalate_to_stylist. The Curator's system prompt names the cases — sympathy, sentimental milestones, deep style coaching — and instructs the agent to always try catalog tools first.

Here, the explicit ask ('a real stylist') tripped the orchestrator's stylist-handoff branch, which routed Anna to recommendation rather than refusing as 'outside shopping.' The Curator chose escalation immediately. That decision is the architecture working — not failing.
